Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with 5 cases We need to model the process as a POWL model Steps 1 Identify the activities OR MS QS AS IQC TST RT PK LB DP D 2 Look at the sequences in the event log to understand the control flow

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process starts with OR Order Received Then MS Material Sourcing and QS Quality Screening follow in order In all cases OR is followed by MS and then QS So we can assume OR then MS then QS are in a fixed order

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After QS we see AS Assembly Then there is a pattern of IQC and AS In some cases there are multiple IQC and AS steps Specifically we see IQC can happen and then AS again and this can repeat multiple IQC and AS cycles

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This suggests that after AS we have an IQC check If the check fails we might have to redo the assembly But note the description of IQC is In line Quality Check repeated if needed So we can model the IQC as a loop that can be repeated until passed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But in Case 4 there is no IQC at all So IQC is optional

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also note the testing phase TST RT TST RT is Re Testing if the product fails the initial test leading back to TST So we have a loop between TST and RT

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then we have PK Packaging LB Labeling which may be skipped DP Distribution Planning and D Dispatch From the event log we see that after the testing phase we have PK then optionally LB then DP and D

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 1 Fixed sequence OR MS QS Step 2 The assembly and quality check loop We can model it as a loop where the body is AS followed by IQC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However in Case 3 we have AS IQC IQC AS This sequence would not be generated by the loop AS IQC because the loop requires an AS after each IQC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternatively we can model the IQC as a separate loop that can be repeated without an AS in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can model the inner loop as IQC tau This means do IQC then either exit or do tau and then IQC again This will generate multiple IQC in a row

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Then the assembly phase can be modeled as AS XOR IQC tau tau This means do AS then either do a loop of IQC or do nothing and then either exit or do another AS and repeat

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This would generate sequences like Case 4 AS then nothing and then exit just AS Case 1 AS IQC AS Case 3 AS IQC IQC AS IQC AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Testing phase We have TST and RT The description says RT leads back to TST So we model it as TST RT

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can model the optional LB as a choice between LB and a silent transition tau So we have XOR LB tau

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The entire process is a partial order that chains these together in sequence OR to MS to QS to AssemblyLoop to TestingLoop to PK to Labeling to DP to D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The event log does not show any concurrency between these steps They are in a fixed sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We have IQCLoop IQC tau AssemblyLoop AS XOR IQCLoop tau TestingLoop TST RT Labeling XOR LB tau Then the root partial order nodes OR MS QS AssemblyLoop TestingLoop PK Labeling DP D order OR MS MS QS QS AssemblyLoop AssemblyLoop TestingLoop TestingLoop PK PK Labeling Labeling DP DP D
